resources.string: See what you\'re owed and what you owe, at a glance. Tap the eye to hide it.
Search or sort your customer list here.
Your customer ledgers — who owes you, and who you owe.
Log your daily cash in and out.
Track cheques you\'ve given or received, with due dates.
Installment plans for customers paying in parts.
Zakat calculator, Dukan Card, backup, App Lock, and settings.
Record what you buy from suppliers and what you still owe them.
Sell it, discount it, or return it — while there is still time.
See what sells, what sits, and where your money is tied up.
A reference aid to estimate your Zakat — confirm the ruling with a qualified scholar.
Make a shareable card for your shop in seconds, in three styles.
Save your ledger safely to your own Google Drive.
Your shop\'s name, contact and preferences — set once, used everywhere.
Quick sums beside your ledger — margins, totals, splits.
Deleted a customer by mistake? Restore them from here.
Tap here to add your first customer and start your khata.
No customers yet.\n\nTap “Add Party” below to add your first customer and start your khata.
Deleted items are kept for 30 days, then removed permanently.
Party (its entries are kept with it)
Restore \"%1$s\"? Its ledger entries will come back too.
Restore this entry? It will reappear in the party\'s ledger and affect the balance.
Everything in the bin will be permanently deleted. This cannot be undone.
\"%1$s\" and its entries will move to the Recycle Bin. You can restore them within 30 days.
This entry will move to the Recycle Bin. You can restore it within 30 days.
Long-press a party to delete it
A reference aid — not a ruling

This tool is only a reference. Zakat has real fiqhi detail — the nisab standard, how doubtful debts are treated, the lunar year, and which liabilities may be deducted all differ between scholars. Roshan Khata is not a mufti. Please take the final ruling from a qualified scholar.
Nisab is set here against the silver standard (612.36 g), the position generally taken as more careful toward the rights of the poor. Enter today\'s silver price per gram.
Enter the price to calculate nisab
Your wealth has reached nisab — Zakat is due (subject to the lunar year being complete).
Your wealth is below nisab — no Zakat is due on this amount.
Cash in hand, bank balances, and the value of trade stock. Roshan Khata cannot see these, so enter them yourself.
Many scholars hold that Zakat on a doubtful debt falls due only once it is actually received. Tick this only if you wish to be more cautious.
Zakat also requires a full lunar year (hawl) to pass over the wealth. Roshan Khata does not track that for you — please account for it yourself, and confirm the ruling with a scholar.
Marked separately from trade. No interest or late fee is ever applied to it.
Read it over before sending. You can edit anything here — Roshan Khata will hand it to WhatsApp or your SMS app, and you press send yourself.

Assalam-o-Alaikum %1$s,

Aap ke zimme %2$s baqi hai. Meherbani farma kar jab mumkin ho ada kar dein. Agar aap ada kar chuke hain to is paighaam ko nazar-andaz karein aur humein bata dein.

(As per our records, %2$s is outstanding on your account. Kindly clear it at your convenience.)

JazakAllah.

—
Sent via Roshan Khata

Assalam-o-Alaikum %1$s,

Hamare zimme aap ka %2$s baqi hai. InshaAllah jald ada kar denge. Agar aap ka hisaab hum se mukhtalif ho to bata dein.

(As per our records, we owe you %2$s. We will settle it soon.)

JazakAllah.

—
Sent via Roshan Khata
No phone number saved for this party. Add one first.
WhatsApp is not installed on this phone.
No SMS app found on this phone.
Nothing outstanding — the account is settled.
If stock moved with this entry — 5 bags of urea, 2 litres of pesticide — record it here. Leave blank for a plain cash entry.
No contacts found on this phone.

Roshan Khata will read your contact list only to show it here, so you can pick who to add. Nothing is uploaded, and only the contacts you actually choose are saved — just their name and number.\n\nIf you would rather not, you can still add every party by hand.
Permission was not granted, so contacts cannot be shown. You can still add parties by hand from the home screen.
Could not create the statement. Please try again.
There are no entries to put in a statement yet.
Your ledger is private. Confirm it is you.
App Lock is on. You will be asked to confirm each time you open the app.
Uses your fingerprint, face, or the screen lock you already have on this phone. Roshan Khata does not store a PIN of its own — Android keeps that safer than we could.
This phone has no screen lock set. Set a fingerprint, PIN, or pattern in your phone\'s settings first, then App Lock can protect your ledger.
App Lock is not available on this phone.
Your name and payment code on statements
Appears at the top of every statement and at the end of every reminder you send.
A screenshot or photo of your EasyPaisa or JazzCash QR code. It will be attached to statements so a customer can scan and pay you.

Check the code carefully before saving. Roshan Khata cannot verify whose account a QR belongs to — if the wrong image is saved, a customer\'s payment will go to the wrong account.\n\nAlso note: a personal EasyPaisa/JazzCash QR does not carry the amount. Your customer will still type it in themselves, so the amount is written clearly alongside.
That image could not be saved. Please pick another.
Look at the code above and confirm it is your own EasyPaisa or JazzCash account. If it is not, your customers\' payments will reach someone else.
No cheques yet.\n\nPost-dated cheques are tracked here separately from the ledger — a cheque is not money until it clears.
A cheque is not money until it clears, so it will not touch the balance yet. When you mark it cleared, the payment is posted to the ledger then.
Pick the date on the cheque
Marking this cleared will post the payment to %1$s\'s ledger and change the balance. Only do this once the money is actually in the account.
A bounced cheque is not a payment — the amount stays owed. Nothing will be posted to the ledger.
Cleared — payment posted to the ledger
Marked as bounced. The amount is still owed.
Pick a customer or supplier from your list first.
Please pick the date written on the cheque.
No customers or suppliers match your search.
Running balance follows the ledger\'s own order, so it stays correct however you sort or search.
That image could not be saved. Please pick another.
The photo stays on this phone. It is never uploaded, and it is deliberately left OFF statements — a statement gets forwarded, and your customer did not agree to their face travelling with it.
The most you are willing to let this party owe you. Roshan Khata will warn you before an entry takes them past it.
Limit (Rs) — leave blank for no limit
This is a warning, not a block. It is your business — you can always proceed anyway.
This will pass their credit limit

%1$s currently owes you %2$s.

This entry of %3$s takes them to %4$s, which is over the %5$s limit you set.

You can still go ahead — this is only a warning.
The three figures above cover only this period. The closing balance below is the full account — %1$s — because a part-period total is not what your customer owes.
The end date cannot be before the start date.
The Cashbook is separate from your customer ledger. Rent, wages, a walk-in cash sale — money with no customer attached. It does not change what anyone owes you.
No cashbook entries yet.\n\nThis is for money that is not tied to a customer — rent, electricity, wages, fuel, a walk-in sale.
It will move to the Recycle Bin.

Everything — customers, ledger, cheques, cashbook, and the Recycle Bin — is written to a single file you can save to Drive, WhatsApp to yourself, or keep on a memory card.\n\nThe file is plain, readable text. Even years from now, even without this app, your records will still be recoverable. Data you cannot get back without our help is not really yours.
Roshan Khata keeps everything on this phone alone. If the phone is lost, broken, or the app is uninstalled, the records go with it. Please take a backup regularly and keep it somewhere else.
Backup ready — choose where to save it
The backup could not be created.

Restoring will REPLACE everything currently in Roshan Khata with what is in this file:

%1$d customers/suppliers
%2$d ledger entries
%3$d cheques
%4$d cashbook entries
%5$d payment plans
%6$d supplier bills

Whatever is on this phone now will be gone. If you have entries here that are not in the backup, take a backup of THIS first.

This cannot be undone.
A plan is an arrangement, not a new debt. What they owe is already in the ledger. Recording a payment here posts it to the ledger once — it is never counted twice.
No payment plans yet.\n\nUse these when a customer will clear an amount in instalments — after the harvest, or across a few months.
Set up an arrangement for clearing what this party already owes. This does not add to their balance — it only records what they have agreed to pay, and when.
This party owes nothing right now
This posts one entry to %1$s\u2019s ledger, reducing what they owe. It is not counted twice.
Payment recorded and posted to the ledger
This payment is more than what is left on the plan (%1$s). It will still be posted in full to the ledger — check the amount before you save.
Plan deleted. Payments already posted to the ledger stay there — the money really was received.
The plan will be removed, but the payments already recorded stay in the ledger. That money really was received, and deleting the arrangement does not undo it.
What you owe a supplier lives in your ledger, same as everything else. A bill adds the paperwork on top: bill number, batch numbers, expiry dates.
No supplier bills yet.\n\nRecord what you buy in — with batch numbers and expiry dates. Those are what an inspector asks for, and what saves you from stock going bad on the shelf.
Tick this only if you paid outright. Otherwise the amount goes on their account in your ledger, which is what you owe them.
This will add %1$s to what you owe %2$s, as one entry in your ledger.

Write the batch number from the container. If a sample is ever questioned, or the Agriculture Department asks, this is the record that shows exactly where the stock came from. It cannot be reconstructed later.
Roshan Khata will warn you 60 days before this expires — early enough to sell it, discount it, or return it while the supplier will still take it.
Bill saved and posted to your ledger
Bill deleted. The ledger entry stays — the stock really did arrive and you really do owe for it.
The bill and its batch records will be removed, but the ledger entry stays. The stock really did arrive and the money is really owed — deleting the paperwork does not undo that.
Sell it, discount it, or return it — while you still can
Nothing expiring in the next 60 days.\n\nOnly stock with an expiry date recorded can be watched. If you leave that field blank on a bill, Roshan Khata cannot warn you about it.
Only stock with an expiry date recorded appears here. Roshan Khata can only warn you about what it was told.
If a sample is questioned or an inspector asks, search the batch number here to see which supplier it came from, on which bill, and when.
No stock found with that batch number.

Saved to:

%1$s

You will find it in your phone’s Downloads folder, and in any file manager. Roshan Khata also keeps its own copy, so you can restore without hunting for the file.
Saved inside Roshan Khata. You can restore it any time from this screen.

A PDF of your whole business \u2014 balances, cheques, plans, expiring stock. For reading, printing, or showing to someone.\n\nThis is NOT a backup. Roshan Khata cannot restore your records from a PDF.
The report could not be created.

Your customers\u2019 names, numbers, and balances are NEVER sent. Only the app version and your phone model go with the report \u2014 that is what helps us find the fault.\n\nThe email opens in your own mail app, so you can see exactly what is being sent before it goes.
A balance or amount looked wrong
I could not understand a screen
Write it in Urdu or English \u2014 whichever is easier. What were you doing when it went wrong?
e.g. I added a cheque of Rs 5,000 and the balance changed straight away, before I cleared it.
This opens your email app. If you have no email set up, write to innovation313.support@gmail.com from any phone.
No email app found. Please write to innovation313.support@gmail.com

Your data is back.\n\n%1$d customer(s) in this backup had been deleted before it was made, so they have been restored to the Recycle Bin \u2014 not the main list. Open More \u2192 Recycle Bin to find them, where you can restore any of them fully.

Connect your Google account and your Khata backs up to your OWN Google Drive \u2014 automatically. Change your phone, sign in again, and it is all back. Your data stays in your Drive; it never goes to us.
Connected as %1$s. No cloud backup yet \u2014 tap \u201CBack up to Drive\u201D.
Backed up to your Google Drive.
Cloud backup failed. Your previous backup, if any, is untouched. Check your internet and try again.
No backup found in your Google Drive yet.
Could not restore from Drive. Check your internet and try again.
Google sign-in did not complete. Please try again.
Roshan Khata needs permission to save to its own folder in your Drive. Please allow it when asked.
This will replace everything currently in the app with the backup from your Google Drive. Continue?
First month of sales \u2014 nothing to compare yet.
No products recorded this month. Add a product name when you make an entry to see this.
No sales recorded this month yet.
All calculated on your phone. Nothing leaves your device.
Silver nisab: 612.36 g (52.5 tola) of silver. This is the lower threshold, so more people owe Zakat \u2014 the position generally taken as more careful toward the rights of the poor.
Gold nisab: 87.48 g (7.5 tola) of gold. This is the higher threshold, so fewer people owe Zakat. Confirm which standard applies to you with a scholar.
This entry will be removed from the ledger.
Could not create the receipt image
You can change the language any time from More.
Cheques due, instalments due, expiring stock and backup nudges
%1$d cheque(s) are due today or overdue.
%1$d payment plan(s) are due for a payment.
%1$d item(s) expire within 60 days. Sell, discount or return them.
Over a week since your last backup. Your records live only on this phone.
A card for your shop, ready to share
The card is drawn on your phone and shared as an image. Nothing is uploaded.
Write to us and we will read it. Tell us what you were doing and what happened — that is usually enough for us to find it.
How do I keep my khata safe if I lose my phone?

Open Backup & Restore from the home screen and sign in with your own Google account. Your ledger is saved to your Drive, where only you can reach it. Do this once a week and a lost phone costs you a phone, not your accounts.
I changed my phone. How do I get my khata back?
Install Roshan Khata on the new phone, open Backup & Restore, and sign in with the same Google account. Your last backup comes back. Anything entered after that backup was made is not in it.
How do I stop others seeing my khata?
More → App Lock. It uses the same fingerprint or PIN your phone already uses, so there is no extra password to remember. Your phone must have a screen lock set for this to work.
Can I hide the balance when someone is standing beside me?
Tap the eye next to the balance on the home screen. It hides everywhere until you tap it again.
Can I work out a discount inside the amount box?
Yes. Type the sum straight in — 1200-15%% gives 1020, and 500*32%% gives 160. It works in every amount field and on the Calculator.
I deleted a customer by mistake.
Recycle Bin, on the home screen. Deleted customers and entries wait there and can be put back.
Can I change the language later?
More → Language, any time. Your ledger is not affected.
Where is my data kept? Can anyone else see it?
On your phone, and nowhere else unless you back it up — and a backup goes to your own Google Drive, not to us. We never see your customers, their numbers, or what anyone owes.
